name: Discord Bot
author: ops@refx.example
description: Host your own Discord bot 24/7. Pick a runtime (Node.js or Python), upload your code via SFTP or the file manager, set your bot token, and go. Auto-restarts on crash.
longDescription: Run any Discord bot on a managed container with full console access, a file manager, and SFTP. Choose your runtime image (Node.js 20/22 or Python 3.11/3.12), set a Startup Command (e.g. "npm install && node index.js" or "pip install -r requirements.txt && python bot.py"), and store your token securely as the BOT_TOKEN environment variable. The bot starts on boot and restarts automatically if it crashes.
startupCommand: bash -c "if [ -f package.json ] && command -v npm >/dev/null 2>&1; then npm install --omit=dev; fi; if [ -f requirements.txt ] && command -v pip >/dev/null 2>&1; then pip install -r requirements.txt; fi; {{STARTUP_CMD}}"

installScript.script:
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
cd /mnt/server
# Seed a placeholder so a fresh server boots and prints instructions until the
# customer uploads their own bot code (via SFTP on port 2022 or the file manager).
if [ ! -e index.js ] && [ ! -e bot.py ] && [ ! -e package.json ] && [ ! -e requirements.txt ]; then
  cat > index.js <<'EOF'
console.log('ReFx bot host is ready. Upload your bot code (index.js + package.json, or bot.py + requirements.txt) via SFTP or the file manager, set your Startup Command + BOT_TOKEN under the server settings, then restart.');
setInterval(function () {}, 1 << 30);
EOF
fi
echo 'Discord bot environment ready.'

variables.description: Command that runs your bot, e.g. "node index.js" or "python bot.py". Dependencies (package.json / requirements.txt) are installed automatically before it runs.
Your Discord bot token. Injected as the BOT_TOKEN environment variable — read it in code (process.env.BOT_TOKEN / os.environ["BOT_TOKEN"]).

If your bot prints a line when it finishes logging in (e.g. "Logged in as" or "Ready!"), enter a snippet of it here. The panel will then show "Starting" until that line appears in the console, then flip to "Running". Leave blank to show "Running" as soon as the container starts.

Your bot's Application ID from the Discord Developer Portal. Many bots use it to register slash commands. Injected as CLIENT_ID — read it as process.env.CLIENT_ID / os.environ["CLIENT_ID"]. Leave blank if your bot doesn't need it.
A Discord server ID, handy for registering slash commands to a single server during development. Injected as GUILD_ID. Leave blank for global commands or if unused.
